reasoning: 8x zoom inspection: sprite is pixel art construction with hard pixel edges and limited palette — no AA blending, no gradient shading, no sub-pixel features, no color bleed into background. (1) White highlight dots on the orange rim are asymmetrically placed — at 8x the left-arc dot count and positioning does not mirror the right arc; a hand-authored pixel artist would typically enforce rotational or bilateral symmetry on a round object highlight. (2) The green cauldron-contents fill has an irregular, blobby silhouette that shows no clear dithering intent and no consistent shape logic — looks closer to noise-fill than deliberate pixel shaping. Neither issue is severe enough alone to fail, but combined they suggest AI-assisted or auto-generated pixel placement rather than fully hand-authored work. Solid areas (black BG, orange body, gray base) are clean with no noise pixels. No pattern tiling artifacts, no melting outlines, no semantic failure — object reads correctly as a cauldron/breakable target. Score 7: marginal.
